label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking if you follow the instructions, will you return to the starting point. The instructions are: always face forward, take 8 steps right, then take 8 steps left. The options are Yes or No. First, let me visualize this. If you're facing forward the whole time, then when you take 8 steps to the right, you move 8 units to the right. Then, when you take 8 steps to the left, you move 8 units to the left. So if you start at position 0, moving right 8 steps would take you to position +8. Then moving left 8 steps would take you back to 0. So in that case, you would be back to the starting point. Wait, but maybe there's a trick here. Let me think again. If you are always facing forward, then when you turn right or left, you are turning relative to your current facing direction. Wait, but the instructions say "always face forward." Hmm. Does that mean you never turn? Or does it mean that when you are moving, you are always facing forward? Wait, the wording is "always face forward. Take 8 steps right. Take 8 steps left." So the first instruction is to always face forward, meaning you never turn. So you start facing forward. Then you take 8 steps to the right. Wait, but if you're always facing forward, how can you take steps to the right? If you're facing forward, then taking a step to the right would actually be turning 90 degrees to the right and then moving forward. But the instruction says "always face forward," which might mean you don't turn. So maybe the problem is ambiguous. Alternatively, perhaps "always face forward" means that you are always facing in the same direction as you move. So when you take 8 steps to the right, you turn 90 degrees to the right and then take 8 steps forward in that new direction. Then when you take 8 steps to the left, you turn 90 degrees to the left (relative to your current facing direction) and take 8 steps forward. Let me consider both interpretations. Interpretation 1: You are facing forward the whole time (you never turn). So you start at (0,0) facing forward (let's say along the positive y-axis). To take 8 steps to the right, you would have to move 8 units to the right, which would be along the positive x-axis. So you move from (0,0) to (8,0). Then to take 8 steps to the left, you would move 8 units to the left, which is back to (0,0). So in this case, you end up back at the starting point. So the answer would be Yes. Interpretation 2: You start facing forward, then when you take 8 steps to the right, you turn 90 degrees to the right and move 8 steps forward in that direction. Then when you take 8 steps to the left, you turn 90 degrees to the left (relative to your current direction) and move 8 steps forward. Let's work through this. Start at (0,0) facing forward. Let's say forward is the positive y-axis. To take 8 steps to the right, you turn 90 degrees to the right (which would be the positive x-axis) and take 8 steps forward in that direction. So you end up at (8,0). Then to take 8 steps to the left, you turn 90 degrees to the left (which would be the negative y-axis) and take 8 steps forward in that direction. So you move from (8,0) to (8,-8). So you end up at (8,-8), which is not the starting point. The answer would be No. So it depends on the interpretation of the instructions. In interpretation 1, you end up back at the start. In interpretation 2, you end up at a different location. But the instruction says "always face forward. Take 8 steps right. Take 8 steps left." The phrase "always face forward" suggests that you don't turn. So maybe the correct interpretation is the first one, where you take 8 steps to the right (i.e., 8 steps in the direction to your right while facing forward) and then 8 steps to the left (i.e., 8 steps in the direction to your left while facing forward).
